tools.purpose: Load bounded Studio Brain startup continuity directly from the host memory control plane before broader repo exploration.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Optional startup query override when the default bootstrap continuity query is not enough.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Fall back to bootstrap artifacts and report that live memory startup context was unavailable.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Load startup continuity and validate Studio Brain reachability before broader repo work.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Block the mission and surface the recovery step from startup continuity.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

lifecycle.retireWhen: Retire once the runtime can call startup continuity directly without shelling through a repo script.

tools.purpose: Audit Codex startup, MCP, telemetry, and local harness health.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Keep the run read-only and require operator review before mutate flows continue.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

lifecycle.retireWhen: Retire once the control plane has first-class doctor and startup diagnostics instead of script orchestration.

tools.purpose: Apply file-backed edit plans in small batches so Windows agents do not depend on oversized inline patch payloads.

inputs.name: --plan
inputs.description: Path to an agent-file-plan.v1 JSON document.

inputs.name: --dry-run
inputs.description: Validate and preview operations without mutating files.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Stop the mutate phase and fall back to smaller file-by-file plans instead of retrying the same oversized payload.
tools.rollbackBehavior: Use expected hashes and the run ledger to reconstruct and restore prior file contents if a bounded file plan misfires.

approvalPolicy.why: Workspace writes are bounded by a file plan and dry-run verifier; high-risk missions still require tool-budget approval.

lifecycle.retireWhen: Retire once Windows-native patch primitives can reliably handle large multi-file payloads without path or transport failures.

tools.purpose: Search Studio Brain memory rows directly through MCP instead of shelling through repo-local HTTP helper scripts.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Focused memory query text such as a feature name, blocker, loop key, or exact error string.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Report the memory bridge failure and fall back to canonical corpus artifacts when appropriate.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Check Studio Brain host reachability directly through the MCP bridge before assuming a memory or control-plane failure is task-specific.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Report that Studio Brain is unreachable and fall back to local continuity artifacts instead of retrying blind.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: List recent Studio Brain memory entries directly from the host control plane.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum number of recent memory rows to return.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Report the recent-memory retrieval failure and continue with bounded local context if available.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Fetch aggregate Studio Brain memory counts and source breakdowns through the host-native MCP bridge.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Report the memory-stats retrieval failure without mutating the run.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Assemble a bounded Studio Brain memory pack for the current task before broader repo exploration.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Fall back to canonical corpus artifacts and note that live memory retrieval was unavailable.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Write durable Studio Brain progress, decisions, blockers, and handoffs through the host memory control plane.

inputs.name: items
inputs.description: Optional batched memory items when saving more than one durable fact or handoff in a turn.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Surface the write failure and keep the run resumable through the local handoff path instead of assuming the save succeeded.
tools.rollbackBehavior: Use memory review surfaces to inspect, quarantine, or reverse suspect writes if a bad save slips through.

approvalPolicy.why: Durable memory writes are allowed for explicit handoffs, blockers, and user-requested saves; speculative personal facts still need human confirmation.

tools.purpose: Repair stale synthetic thread metadata through the Studio Brain host control plane instead of running laptop-side memory hygiene loops.

inputs.name: dryRun
inputs.description: Inspect eligible repairs without applying them.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Stop after the dry-run wave and require operator review before any live repair pass.
tools.rollbackBehavior: Use memory review and repair receipts to reverse or quarantine suspect metadata writes.

approvalPolicy.why: Memory hygiene can rewrite durable metadata and should remain human-approved after dry-run evidence.

tools.purpose: Execute memory consolidation on the Ubuntu Studio Brain host through the MCP/control plane instead of local database access.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Consolidation mode such as idle or overnight.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Stop and surface the host-side auth or control-plane error instead of retrying local database access.
tools.rollbackBehavior: Use the resulting consolidation artifact and memory review surfaces to quarantine or revert suspect follow-on writes if needed.

approvalPolicy.why: Host-side consolidation is an established maintenance primitive, but artifacts must remain reviewable.

tools.purpose: List Studio Brain loop incidents directly from the host control plane for ops triage and blocker identification.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Optional focused incident query, owner, loop key, or lane filter.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Report that loop-incident retrieval failed and continue with bounded local context or direct repo evidence.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Build a bounded Studio Brain loop action plan through the host-native MCP bridge instead of reconstructing one from ad hoc script flows.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Optional action-plan focus query or owner lane hint.

tools.safeFailBehavior: Report the action-plan retrieval failure and keep the run in a bounded analysis state instead of improvising a broad ops sweep.
tools.rollbackBehavior: No rollback required; this surface is read-only.

tools.purpose: Run the repo PR gate before claiming a change is ready for publish or review.
tools.safeFailBehavior: Keep the mission blocked and attach the PR gate output as verifier evidence.

lifecycle.retireWhen: Retire once PR gating is exposed as a first-class native capability with structured results.
